title: Generic runtime-bridge — delegate(task_spec) -> {summary, artifacts} contract (stdin spec, allowlist, artifact write, schema-validated return, delegation log, retry-once)

tests.summary: 6 passed — validateTaskSpec rejects missing task + out-of-allowlist model; parseTaskSpec fails loudly on invalid JSON; delegate runs the secondary via its RuntimeAdapter, writes FULL output to the Artifact Store, returns a schema-valid {summary, artifacts[]}; logs the delegation; loud locked-vs-absent missing-key; retry-once-then-fail (initial + 1 retry).

probe.summary: ok — the Codex runtime-bridge entrypoint health-checks the codex binary + prints ok (codex CLI present).

build.summary: unchanged — packages/claude-pty .mjs + fitting scripts, not the Next bundle

e2e.summary: N/A — bridge contract; committed unit tests + the codex bridge --probe are the re-runnable gate.

notes: packages/claude-pty/src/runtime-bridge.mjs is the generic, runtime-agnostic bridge CORE built on the RuntimeAdapter contract. The channel is MCP (the per-runtime fitting wraps this core in a stdio MCP server / CLI); the core's delegate() runs the secondary in ITS OWN loop via the adapter (a pooled TUI session for Claude-as-secondary — never -p), writes full output to the Artifact Store, returns a schema-validated summary + artifact paths, and appends the delegation to decisions.jsonl. Hardening: task spec via stdin/temp (parseTaskSpec, never argv), model per-provider allowlist, loud DelegationError distinguishing vault-locked vs secret-absent, retry-once-then-fail-loudly. The Codex bridge (fittings/seed/codex-runtime/scripts/bridge.mjs) wraps it with --probe + artifacts.py write + decisions.jsonl. Satisfies brief §3 token runtime-bridge-ok.
